TextView.android:text:
Nutrition is the fundamental process by which the body obtains and utilizes essential nutrients from food to support growth, maintain bodily functions, and provide energy.

 Core Benefits of Proper Nutrition

1. Disease Prevention: A balanced diet significantly reduces the risk of chronic non-communicable diseases, including heart disease (WHO), type 2 diabetes, stroke, and certain types of cancer.

 2. Immune System Support: Nutrients such as Vitamin C, Vitamin D, Vitamin A, zinc, and iron are vital for strengthening the immune system and helping the body fight off infections.

 3.Physical Growth and Maintenance: Proteins serve as building blocks for muscles, organs, and tissues, while minerals like calcium and Vitamin D are essential for developing and maintaining strong bones and teeth (CDC).

 4.Energy and Performance: Carbohydrates are the primary fuel source for the body and brain, supporting daily activities and stabilizing energy levels to prevent fatigue.

 5.Mental Health and Brain Function: Essential fats like Omega-3 fatty acids and B vitamins support cognitive health, enhance memory, and can help regulate mood and reduce the risk of mental health disorders like depression.

 6.Longevity and Quality of Life: Adopting healthy eating patterns early can increase life expectancy and help maintain independence and vitality as you age.

 Practical Tips for Better Nutrition

 i)Half-Plate Rule: Aim to fill half your plate with colorful fruits and vegetables at most meals.

 ii)Limit Processed Foods: Reduce intake of ultra-processed foods that are typically high in added sugars, sodium, and unhealthy trans fats.

 iii)Stay Hydrated: Water is critical for nearly every bodily function, including nutrient transport and temperature regulation.Aim for 6–8 glasses daily. 

 iv)Choose Whole Grains: Opt for brown rice, oats, and whole-wheat varieties over refined white flour products to ensure higher fiber and nutrient intake.
